title: Tracing and Span Management
description: Agent runs emit trace spans; processors record and export observability data to OpenAI backend or custom destinations.

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Creates traces manually via trace(), wraps workflows, registers custom processors, disables tracing, calls flush_traces()

actors.name: Runner
actors.description: Automatically creates traces (TraceCtxManager) and emits spans per agent run, agent loop turn, LLM generation, tool call, handoff, guardrail, audio

actors.name: Agent
actors.description: Emits AgentSpanData, GenerationSpanData, FunctionSpanData, GuardrailSpanData, HandoffSpanData as part of run lifecycle

actors.name: OpenAI Backend
actors.description: Receives batched span/trace payloads via POST https://api.openai.com/v1/traces/ingest (Bearer token auth)

actors.name: Custom Exporter
actors.description: Third-party tracing backend (W&B, Langfuse, MLflow, etc.) receiving traces via TracingProcessor/TracingExporter interface

use_cases.name: Create and Manage Trace
use_cases.description: Call trace(workflow_name, trace_id?, group_id?, metadata?, disabled?) as context manager or manually start()/finish(). Trace stored in contextvar via Scope.
use_cases.notes: TraceCtxManager wraps this for Runner-initiated traces; reattach_resumed_trace=True reuses existing trace_id across resumed runs

use_cases.name: Create Typed Span
use_cases.description: Call *_span() helpers (agent_span, generation_span, function_span, guardrail_span, handoff_span, custom_span, transcription_span, speech_span) which delegate to DefaultTraceProvider.create_span(span_data).
use_cases.notes: Parent resolved from Scope (current_span / current_trace) if not provided. Returns NoOpSpan when tracing disabled.

use_cases.name: Start and Finish Span Lifecycle
use_cases.description: SpanImpl.start() records started_at, calls processor.on_span_start(). SpanImpl.finish() records ended_at, calls processor.on_span_end(). Context manager automates this.

use_cases.name: Record Span Error
use_cases.description: span.set_error({message, data}) attaches SpanError to SpanImpl. Exported in span payload under 'error' field.

use_cases.name: Export Trace/Span Data
use_cases.description: Background worker thread drains queue every 5s (schedule_delay) or when queue > 70% full (export_trigger_ratio). Calls TracingExporter.export(items) with batches of up to 128 items.
use_cases.notes: Traces enqueued on on_trace_start; spans enqueued on on_span_end. Max queue 8192. Drops items when full.

use_cases.name: Send Traces to OpenAI Backend
use_cases.description: HTTP POST to https://api.openai.com/v1/traces/ingest with Bearer API key. Exponential backoff retry (max 3, base 1s, max 30s). Sanitizes large fields (truncate >100KB) and invalid usage data before sending.
use_cases.notes: Items grouped by tracing_api_key. 4xx = no retry. 5xx = retry.

use_cases.name: Register Additional Trace Processor
use_cases.description: Call add_trace_processor(processor) to append to SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor. Original processors (BatchTraceProcessor→BackendSpanExporter) remain active.

use_cases.name: Replace Default Trace Processors
use_cases.description: Call set_trace_processors([...]) to replace all processors. Traces no longer sent to OpenAI unless a custom processor handles it.

use_cases.name: Flush Traces Immediately
use_cases.description: Call flush_traces() to block until all buffered traces/spans exported. Propagates via DefaultTraceProvider.force_flush() → SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor.force_flush() → BatchTraceProcessor.force_flush() → _export_batches(force=True).
use_cases.notes: Use after trace() context exits in long-running workers (Celery, FastAPI background tasks)

use_cases.name: Disable Tracing
use_cases.description: Three methods: (1) env var OPENAI_AGENTS_DISABLE_TRACING=1, (2) set_tracing_disabled(True) via DefaultTraceProvider.set_disabled(), (3) RunConfig.tracing_disabled=True for single run. Returns NoOpTrace/NoOpSpan when disabled.

use_cases.name: Reattach Resumed Trace
use_cases.description: TraceCtxManager with reattach_resumed_trace=True calls reattach_trace(trace_state) if matching trace_id was previously started and settings match (workflow_name, group_id, metadata, api_key hash).

use_cases.name: Print Traces to Console
use_cases.description: Register ConsoleSpanExporter with a BatchTraceProcessor for local debugging. Prints trace_id/name or span.export() dict.

key_relationships: DefaultTraceProvider composes SynchronousMultiTracingProcessor (fan-out to N processors)
SpanImpl.start() sets Scope.current_span via contextvar token
NoOpTrace/NoOpSpan implement Trace/Span interfaces as null objects
SpanData subclasses (AgentSpanData, GenerationSpanData, etc.) implement SpanData.export()
setup.py GLOBAL_TRACE_PROVIDER lazily initialized with DefaultTraceProvider + default_processor()
span_data_types: TurnSpanData (turn, agent_name, usage) — agent loop iteration
SpeechSpanData (input text, output PCM, model, first_content_at)
important_notes: All context propagation uses Python contextvars (Scope class) — async-safe automatically
BatchTraceProcessor uses daemon background thread started lazily on first span/trace
BackendSpanExporter groups exports by tracing_api_key; supports per-trace API keys
Sensitive data (LLM I/O, audio PCM) controlled by RunConfig.trace_include_sensitive_data and VoicePipelineConfig.trace_include_sensitive_audio_data
BackendSpanExporter sanitizes: truncates input/output fields >100KB, strips non-allowed usage keys, drops usage from non-generation spans
atexit handler registered once to call provider.shutdown() on process exit
ZDR (Zero Data Retention) orgs cannot use tracing
